summary: Matched src/melee/gm/gmscdata.c by reconstructing the game-mode scene data tables, switching gmscdata.c from NonMatching to Matching, and tightening many scene callback declarations across gm, mn, ty, un, and vi headers. The main data recovery was replacing extern declarations for gm_803DA920 and gm_803DACA4 with concrete MinorSceneHandler and MajorScene arrays containing OnFrame/OnEnter/OnLeave callbacks and minor-scene table pointers. The PR also corrected gm_803DACA4's symbol size from 0x39C to 0x398 and changed gm_801A50AC to return gm_803DACA4 instead of &gm_803DACA4. There was no human review text; decomp.dev reported linked code unchanged overall but a +24 byte improvement and a gmcamera unmatched-function improvement.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so Object(NonMatching, "melee/gm/gmscdata.c") became Object(Matching, "melee/gm/gmscdata.c").
In gmscdata.c, replaced extern declarations for gm_803DA920 and gm_803DACA4 with concrete data definitions: static MinorSceneHandler gm_803DA920[] and MajorScene gm_803DACA4[].

Populated gm_803DA920 with minor scene handler entries keyed by IDs such as 0x00 through 0x2D, wiring callbacks from title, menu, character select, stage select, un, vi, trophy, gm_1A4C, and camera code.

Populated gm_803DACA4 with major scene entries containing flags/IDs, OnLoad/OnUnload/OnInit callbacks, and pointers to existing MinorScene arrays such as gm_803DD6A0_MinorScenes through gm_803DFE48_MinorScenes.
Changed gm_801A50AC to return gm_803DACA4 rather than &gm_803DACA4, matching the new array definition.
Adjusted config/GALE01/symbols.txt for gm_803DACA4 size from 0x39C to 0x398.
Updated many scene callback prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete void signatures: OnFrame(void), OnEnter(UNK_T), and OnLeave(UNK_T).
Updated implementations of empty leave/enter functions to accept unused UNK_T where needed, including gm_801AC6D8_OnEnter, gm_801ACC90_OnLeave, gmCamera_801A367C_OnLeave, and un_802FED10_OnLeave.
Added or expanded header declarations needed by the scene tables, especially gmtitle.h, tydisplay.h, vi0401.h, vi0801.h, vi1201v2.h, and vi1202.h.
Moved some declarations out of un_2FC9.h into more specific vi/ty headers, evidenced by un_80322258 and related vi1202 symbols being removed from un_2FC9.h and added to vi1202.h.

Added include dependencies in files that used symbols relocated or newly declared in vi1202.h and tydisplay.h, including ftCo_Damage.c, ftCo_FallSpecial.c, fighter.c, ftcommon.c, gm_1A3F.c, and ground.c.
smart_moves: Matched a data-heavy object by reconstructing the scene tables directly instead of only stubbing extern symbols.
Normalized callback prototypes before assigning them into MinorSceneHandler tables, reducing the need for unsafe casts and making table intent clearer.
Used explicit OnFrame/OnEnter/OnLeave suffixes consistently with the callback role in the scene data.
Kept necessary casts only where signatures still did not line up, such as (void*) vi0102_Initialize_OnEnter and casts around gm_801B0264_OnEnter/gm_801B0304_OnLeave in the table.
Corrected the symbol size metadata for gm_803DACA4 alongside the source reconstruction, avoiding a stale data-size annotation.

Separated declarations into more local headers such as vi1202.h and tydisplay.h so the reconstructed gmscdata.c could include the owning modules rather than relying on unrelated catch-all declarations.
decomp_lessons: For data-only or data-heavy game-mode objects, matching may require reconstructing global arrays exactly, including sentinel/null entries, callback ordering, and pointer-to-minor-scene-table layout.
When a symbol is actually an array, downstream accessors should return the array pointer directly; returning &array can encode the wrong type/address expression and hurt matching.

Scene callback tables expose true function signatures. If table slots expect void OnFrame(void), void OnEnter(UNK_T), and void OnLeave(UNK_T), update headers and trivial implementations accordingly instead of leaving everything as UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Header cleanup can be a prerequisite for data matching: data tables require every referenced function to have a visible compatible declaration.
Moving prototypes to domain-specific headers helps reveal ownership: vi1202-related functions belong in vi1202.h, trophy display functions in tydisplay.h, etc.
Symbol file object sizes should be revisited when reconstructing arrays; the PR changed gm_803DACA4 from 0x39C to 0x398 based on the matched data layout.
Use UNK_T unused parameters for scene callbacks whose implementations do not consume their state argument, preserving callback ABI without inventing a struct type.
naming_conventions: Scene callback names use address plus role suffix, e.g. gm_801A632C_OnEnter, gm_801A64A8_OnFrame, gm_801A0B18_OnLeave.
OnFrame callbacks were declared as void function(void) when no argument is used.
OnEnter and OnLeave callbacks were declared as void function(UNK_T) when the scene system passes an opaque argument.
Minor scene arrays follow names like gm_803DD6A0_MinorScenes, preserving address plus _MinorScenes suffix.
Header guards were modernized in some headers to MELEE subsystem-style names, e.g. MELEE_GM_TITLE_H, MELEE_TY_DISPLAY_H, MELEE_VI_0401_H, MELEE_VI_0801_H, and MELEE_VI_1201_V2_H.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promote gmscdata.c to Matching only after defining the actual data tables rather than leaving gm_803DA920/gm_803DACA4 as extern.
Make callback table prototypes exact enough that function pointers can be emitted in the same table form as the original binary.
Use static for gm_803DA920 in gmscdata.c while exporting MajorScene gm_803DACA4[] as a global data symbol.
For incompatible known callbacks, use targeted (void*) casts in the initializer rather than broadening every prototype.
Adjust source and symbol metadata together when array shape changes produce a different object size.
Add includes in dependent C files after moving prototypes to specific headers so compilation order remains explicit.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
decomp.dev reported for GALE01: linked code 22.99% (+0.00%, +24 bytes), with one unmatched-function improvement in main/melee/gm/gmcamera: gmCamera_801A2334 improved from 52.31% to 59.23%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename gm_803DA920 or gm_803DACA4 to semantic scene table names.
Search for remaining scene callbacks still declared as UNK_RET or UNK_PARAMS after this PR.
Inspect MajorScene and MinorSceneHandler struct definitions to document the exact field meanings used by gmscdata.c.
Look for later cleanup of the remaining (void*) casts in gmscdata.c scene initializers.
Check whether the static function declarations added to headers like gmtitle.h and vi0401.h were later moved or corrected.
